constraints: Sum of aij for each i equals 1
content.inputFormat: First line contains T, the number of test cases. Each test case contains two integers N and K, where N is the number of words in the list and K is the number of clappings. The next line contains a string S, the initial word. The following 26 lines contain 26 space-separated doubles, representing the probability that each letter transforms into another letter after clapping. The following N lines contain strings si, the words in the list.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the probability that after exactly K clappings, the initial word will be equal to one of the words in the list.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Explanation: daa (a->d) * (b->a) * (c->a) | 0.25*0.5*0.1 = 0.0125, bbb (a->b) * (b->b) * (c->b) | 0.25*0.5*0.9 = 0.1125, ccc (a->c) * (b->c) * (c->c) | 0, zz (a->z) * (b->z) * (c can't transform to empty space as it is not allowed).

solution.approach: The problem can be solved by first calculating the probability of transformation of each letter in the initial word to each letter in the words in the list after K clappings. This can be done by exponentiating the transformation matrix K times. Then, for each word in the list, calculate the probability that the initial word transforms into that word after K clappings by multiplying the probabilities of transformation of each letter.
